page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-64 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 256 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong capabilities in memory operations and specific compute tasks. The instance achieves strong performance in large-block read-only memory bandwidth at 268.78 GB/sec and large-block write memory bandwidth at 131.67 GB/sec. Passmark database operations and Passmark memory scores are also in the strong tier. However, single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench ray tracing, fall into the average category. Large-scale LLM text generation with a 70B model is a notable performance bottleneck, registering in the weak category at 2.67 tokens/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-standard-64 offers a balanced resource density for enterprise workloads that require high memory bandwidth and substantial CPU threads without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. The hardware configuration is suited for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving. It is less optimal for large-scale LLM text generation due to the lack of hardware acceleration and lower performance in high-parameter model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-64 is an x86_64 general-purpose server configured with 64 vCPUs, 32 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 2.8 GHz, and 256 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance relies on its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Performance benchmarks show strong results in large-block memory bandwidth, software compilation with Clang, and Passmark database operations. Conversely, it exhibits average performance in standard single-core and multi-core CPU tests, and weak performance in large-model LLM text generation. This profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bandwidth-sensitive workloads, database operations, and software development pipelines, while being less suitable for heavy AI inference tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-64 is a general-purpose server with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong memory bandwidth and database performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-64 features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, offering strong memory bandwidth for database workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and strong memory bandwidth for database and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM powered by 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processors
Strong memory bandwidth performance for large-block read and write operations
Well-suited for software compilation and database operations
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
Weak performance for large-scale 70B LLM text generation tasks
